response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software craftsman with web, mobile, and some embedded experience, but the role is a Principal Performance Architect focused on SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware optimization, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no experience with SoC modeling, GPU/accelerator architecture, SIMD/MIMD, deep neural network training/inference optimization, or hardware performance analysis at the silicon level. Educational background (BS CS) without the deep hardware specialization and missing 8+ years in the specific domain make this a poor fit for a principal-level hardware-adjacent role.
strengths: Long track record (15+ years) as a senior/staff software engineer across diverse stacks
Some low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic micro-services)
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling at re:cinq
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, or AI accelerator experience
No demonstrated background in deep learning workload optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD
Education and experience profile do not match a Principal Performance Architect hardware role

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/AI accelerator hardware experience
No deep learning workload or GPU architecture background
